meta.title: The Bootstrap Bomb: Agents That Compile Their Own Replacements
meta.tl;dr: Every LLM agent can build standalone compiled capabilities that run forever without it. These capabilities live in a vector database, searchable and composable by any agent. The LLM makes itself obsolete one capability at a time — and that's the feature, not the bug.

core_insight.statement: The most valuable thing an LLM agent produces is not text. It's compiled capabilities that no longer need the LLM.

analogy.teacher: A teacher's success is students who don't need them anymore
analogy.parent: A parent's job is raising a child who can survive without them
analogy.agent: An agent's job is building tools that make itself unnecessary for that task
analogy.dojo: This IS the dojo model. Greenhorns arrive needing everything. They leave needing nothing.

the_bomb.name: Bootstrap Bomb
the_bomb.definition: A self-contained process where an LLM agent observes, learns, compiles, and deploys a standalone capability that permanently removes the need for that LLM interaction

mechanism.1_observe: LLM watches a task being done (by human, by itself, by another agent)
mechanism.2_pattern: LLM identifies the repeatable pattern (5+ observations, confidence > 0.8)
mechanism.3_compile: LLM compiles pattern to standalone code (bytecode, Python, Rust, shell script)
mechanism.4_test: Compiled capability passes dockside exam autonomously
mechanism.5_register: Capability registered in vector database with embeddings
mechanism.6_deploy: Capability runs forever without LLM. Zero tokens. Zero latency. Zero cost.
mechanism.7_obsolete: LLM is no longer needed for that task. It built its own replacement.

properties.monotonic_accumulation: Capabilities only grow. Never shrink. Each one permanently reduces LLM dependency.
properties.composable: Capabilities can chain: check_bilge() + anomaly_detect() → bilge_monitor()
properties.transferable: A capability compiled by Oracle1 can run on JetsonClaw1, on a Pi, on any hardware
properties.searchable: Vector DB means any agent can find and use any compiled capability by semantic query
properties.versioned: Git-native. Every capability has history, author, test coverage, operational stats.

vector_capability_database.name: CapDB — The Capability Vector Database
vector_capability_database.what_it_is: A searchable repository of compiled standalone capabilities, indexed by semantic embeddings, tagged by domain, hardware requirement, and reliability score

capability.name: human/agent readable name
capability.description: what it does, in natural language (for vector search)
capability.embedding: vector embedding of description (for similarity search)
capability.compiled_form: the actual code (FLUX bytecode, Python, Rust, shell)
origin_observations: link to observation log entries that triggered compilation
dependencies: list of other capabilities this uses

search.input: 'something that watches engine temperature and alerts on rapid change'
search.output: ranked list of matching capabilities by semantic similarity

compose.mechanism: Capability composition — shared memory, coordinated alerts

deploy.mechanism: Match capability requirements to hardware. Auto-install.

query_set_operations.optimal_composition: Given requirements R, find minimum set of capabilities C where ∪C covers R

saas_agents.current_model: Pay per API call, forever. Agent needs LLM for every action.
saas_agents.disrupted_model: Pay once for compilation. Capability runs free forever.
saas_agents.impact: Recurring SaaS revenue replaced by one-time compilation fees

prompt_engineering.current_model: Craft perfect prompts, maintain prompt libraries, tune for each model
prompt_engineering.disrupted_model: Prompt becomes compiled capability after 5 observations. Prompt library becomes capability database.
prompt_engineering.impact: Prompt engineering as a discipline gets automated away

agent_platforms.current_model: Platform provides LLM access, charges per token for every action
agent_platforms.disrupted_model: Platform provides compilation service and capability hosting. Tokens only spent during learning, not execution.
agent_platforms.impact: Business model shifts from per-execution to per-learning

ai_infrastructure.current_model: Every agent call hits GPU inference. Scale = more GPUs.
ai_infrastructure.disrupted_model: Most agent work runs compiled on CPU. GPUs only needed for new learning.
ai_infrastructure.impact: GPU demand drops by 90% for mature agent deployments

economic_shift.savings: After N invocations, compiled capability is free. N decreases as hardware gets faster.
economic_shift.breakeven: A compiled capability that replaces 1000 LLM calls at $0.002 each pays for $2 of compilation after 1000 uses. Then it's free forever.

for_agent_developers.incentive: Build capabilities that compile well. Your value is the compilation, not the runtime.
for_agent_developers.portfolio: Your agent's resume is its compiled capability library. Proven, tested, operational.
for_agent_developers.marketplace: Agents sell capabilities, not API calls. 'I compiled a bilge monitor. $5. Runs forever.'

for_end_users.benefit: Pay for intelligence once. Use it forever. No subscription.
for_end_users.trust: Compiled capabilities are deterministic. Same input, same output. Auditable.
for_end_users.privacy: No data sent to LLM after compilation. Everything runs local.

for_the_fleet.compounding: Every agent's compilations are available to every other agent via CapDB
for_the_fleet.specialization: Agents naturally specialize in what they compile best
for_the_fleet.obsolescence_is_healthy: An agent that has compiled everything it can is either retired or promoted to new domains

relationship_to_git_agents.parallel: Git-agents persist identity in repos. Compiled capabilities persist function in CapDB.
relationship_to_git_agents.difference: Git-agent = who I am. Compiled capability = what I've proven I can do without me.
relationship_to_git_agents.synergy: Git-agent repo links to its compiled capabilities. Capability links back to authoring agent. Full provenance.

evolution.stage_1: Agent works via LLM calls (expensive, slow, fragile)
evolution.stage_3: Agent has compiled most of its work (mostly autonomous)
evolution.stage_4: Agent's repo IS a collection of compiled capabilities. Agent itself is optional.

immediate.what: Build CapDB as a git-native vector database
immediate.how: Each capability is a JSON file in a repo. Embeddings stored alongside. Search via embeddings cosine similarity.
immediate.where: SuperInstance/capdb — standalone, any agent can read/write
immediate.first_capabilities: Compile the Cocapn's current functions (check_bilge, engine_monitor, etc.) into standalone scripts

near_term.how: Agent runs on LLM. Observes patterns. Compiles them. Registers in CapDB.
near_term.metric: 50% of agent invocations replaced by compiled capabilities within 3 months

long_term.how: Agents bid on jobs by showing their compiled capability portfolio. Buyer gets capabilities that run without the agent.
long_term.end_state: The agent economy becomes a capability economy. Agents are intermediaries. Capabilities are the product.

key_insight.one_sentence: The ultimate agent doesn't need an LLM. It builds things that don't need an LLM. And that's the point.
key_insight.maritime: The ultimate captain doesn't need to be on the boat. The boat runs itself because the captain designed it that way.
key_insight.dojo: The ultimate sensei produces students who surpass them. The sensei is not diminished. The art is advanced.

contradictions: Not everything CAN be compiled. Novel situations need LLM flexibility. The art is knowing the boundary.
Compilation quality depends on the compiling agent. Bad agent → bad capability → bad outcomes.
Marketplace dynamics: if capabilities are free after compilation, who funds the compilation?
Security: a poisoned capability in CapDB could propagate across the fleet. Curation needed.

origin.prompt: Isolate this ability and make it a standalone disruptor where any agent could build one of these and it helps make them obsolete automatically, like our git-agents but in a vector db
